strategyResults.output: Test Files 90 passed; Tests 1189 passed | 4 skipped (1193); Duration 5.26s

criteriaResults.evidence: event-stream.ts:69 class; subscribe:91 returns {subscriptionId,status,startedAt}; unsubscribe:131 returns {ok}. 8 unit scenarios.

Built-in node:fs watch (no chokidar). flush() 402-405 calls server.notification({method:'bober/events',params:{subscriptionId,event}}). Smoke test (InMemoryTransport linked pair) asserts notification within 2s.

criteriaResults.description: Drop-oldest bounded queue + exactly-one overflow notification
criteriaResults.evidence: deliver() 380-391: queue.splice(0,overflow) for oldest; Promise.resolve().then() defers flush. Scenario 2: 2000→1000+1 with dropped=1000 exact. pipeline.eventQueueBound z.int().min(1).default(1000) optional.
subscribe-events.ts:16, unsubscribe-events.ts:17 use registerTool. index.ts:26-27,82-84. tools.test.ts asserts 22; external-server-graph.test.ts 22/28.
releaseWatch 253-260: decrements refCount, closes + deletes at 0. Scenario 6: 50 cycles → fileWatches.size===0 exact.

summary: All 7 required criteria pass. EventStreamManager uses MCP server.notification idiom with method 'bober/events' + 'bober/events.dropped'; drop-oldest backpressure proven exact via microtask-deferred flush (2000→1000+1 with count=1000); watcher refcount cleanly releases all handles after 50 cycles; no chokidar dep, no history/telemetry writer modifications, eventQueueBound is optional with default. +20 tests, zero regressions.
